news.title: Unauthorised layouts mushroom near Mulapeta port
news.para: The under-construction Mulapeta Port in Srikakulam has fueled a real estate boom, leading to numerous unauthorized land layouts. Realtors are allegedly converting agricultural land into plots without proper permissions, often failing to provide basic amenities. Many buyers are being lured by promises of high returns on plots in these unapproved ventures.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh: APMDC plans to acquire more land for beach sand mining
news.para: Andhra Pradesh Mineral Development Corporation is seeking an additional 5,000 hectares for beach sand mining in Srikakulam, following the Centre's earlier allocation of 1,000 hectares. With operations underway, APMDC is pushing for the next phase. Some proposed sites are in reserve forests, requiring compensatory afforestation. APMDC plans to use previously acquired land for this purpose.

news.title: ED arrests businessman in sand mining scam in West Bengal
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate has taken a bold step in the battle against sand smuggling in West Bengal, arresting businessman Arun Sharaf, who leads GD Mining Company. Facing grave accusations of illegal sand mining and money laundering, officers uncovered that his company had been operating beyond sanctioned extraction limits.

news.title: Nissan Motor sells, leases back headquarters in $643 million deal
news.para: Nissan has finalized a significant deal to sell and lease back its Yokohama global headquarters. This 97 billion yen transaction is a key part of the automaker's business restructuring. The company anticipates booking substantial extraordinary income from this sale in the current fiscal year.

news.title: Mumbai: MMRDA invites bids to lease ₹1,629-crore Wadala plot
news.para: MMRDA has invited bids for a 10,860 sq m commercial plot in Wadala, with a reserve price of Rs 1,629 crore, aiming to monetize land outside the saturated Bandra-Kurla Complex. This move marks a significant step in developing Wadala as Mumbai's third central business district, boosting non-fare revenue for infrastructure projects.

news.title: Property tax camps in Gurugram zone III from Nov 8 to help clear dues
news.para: Gurugram's Municipal Corporation (MCG) is organizing property tax camps across Zone 3 colonies from November 8 to November 30. These camps aim to help residents clear dues, correct property details, and obtain on-site self-certification. Citizens are advised to bring Aadhaar, PAN cards, and property ownership documents for verification.

news.title: Ghaziabad: HC sets Indirapuram Extension land rate for farmers, clears 20-year-old hurdle
news.para: Ghaziabad Development Authority must pay enhanced compensation to 35 farmers for Indirapuram Extension township land. The Allahabad High Court ruled that compensation should be based on 2014 rates. This decision settles a two-decade-old land dispute. Farmers have the option to retain their land or sell it at the new rates.

news.title: Five societies in Thane claim Rajshila Construction LLP used their FSI for new towers
news.para: Petition before high court says Rajshila Construction misused their property rights and Thane municipality wrongly approved the developer’s new project plans without verifying whether existing housing societies had given consent

news.title: Navi Mumbai: Cidco tender for 10-acre Kharghar plot attracts record ₹2,120 crore bid
news.para: Aakar Developers has secured a 10-acre plot in Kharghar's Sector 23 for a record Rs 2,120 crore, marking the biggest land deal since Nerul-Seawoods. This high bid reflects Kharghar's growing appeal due to the Navi Mumbai airport, universities, and golf course, driving strong demand for premium housing projects.

news.title: SC sets December 5 deadline for Jaypee Infratech homebuyers' claims
news.para: The Supreme Court has given Jaypee Infratech homebuyers a December 5 deadline to claim refunds or apply for flat allotments. Suraksha Realty, now managing the stalled projects, must entertain these claims, with no further extensions granted. This directive follows years of uncertainty for over 20,000 stranded buyers.

news.title: Zomato Hyperpure leases 5.5 lakh sq ft warehouse in Bhiwandi near Mumbai
news.para: Zomato Hyperpure has secured a significant warehousing space in Bhiwandi, near Mumbai. This move strengthens their backend operations for supplying essentials to restaurants. The company leased over 550,000 sq ft for nearly five years. This expansion highlights the growing demand for logistics facilities in the region. Bhiwandi is a key hub for e-commerce and logistics companies.

news.title: HCC's net profit declines 25% to ₹47.78 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Hindustan Construction Company (HCC) faced a setback in the September quarter, with net profits plummeting by more than 25% compared to last year and a decrease in overall income. Yet, the silver lining comes with the acquisition of new contracts worth Rs 2,770 crore, along with being the most competitive bidder for an Rs 840 crore project.

news.title: Smartworks' loss narrows to ₹3 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Smartworks Coworking Spaces Ltd is turning the tide, reporting a diminished net loss of Rs 3.13 crore for Q2, despite facing higher expenses. The company experienced a remarkable 22 percent jump in total income.

news.title: Embassy Developments posts loss of ₹152.61 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Embassy Developments reported a net consolidated loss of ₹152.61 crore for Q2 FY26, despite total income of ₹536.90 crore. The company secured ₹1,370 crore in growth capital and is set to launch new projects in Bengaluru and Mumbai, aiming to achieve its ₹5,000 crore FY26 pre-sales guidance.

news.title: Akzo Nobel India posts profit of ₹1,682.80 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Akzo Nobel India announced a substantial profit of ₹1,682.80 crore for the quarter ending September 30, 2025. This marks a significant increase from the previous year's ₹97.90 crore. The company's total income saw a slight decrease. Despite challenges, disciplined cost control helped maintain profitability.

news.title: Toronto home sales fall to four-month low in October 2025
news.para: In October, the Greater Toronto Area experienced a significant drop in home sales, touching a four-month low. The primary reason for this decline appears to be buyer reluctance fueled by an unpredictable economic landscape. Although the home price index registered a slight monthly increase, it remains lower than last year.

news.title: Bajaj Housing Finance's net profit up 17.84% in Q2 FY26
news.para: Bajaj Housing Finance announced a significant 17.84 percent rise in its net  profit for the quarter ending September 30, 2025. The company's profit after tax reached ₹642.96 crore. Total income also saw a healthy increase of 14.30 percent. Assets under management grew by 24 percent, demonstrating strong business expansion.

news.title: Sweden's Skanska operating profit rises to $148.7 million in Q3 2025
news.para: Skanska, the prominent Swedish builder, faced a challenging quarter as its third-quarter earnings fell below analyst predictions. The company's operating profits took a hit from significant property write-downs and subdued demand in the residential development arena, predominantly in Sweden. However, in an optimistic note, Skanska emphasized that the broader construction market outlook remains relatively steady.

news.title: JK Lakshmi Cement posts profit of ₹80.63 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: JK Lakshmi Cement has achieved a profit of ₹80.63 crore in the September quarter, a strong recovery from last year's loss. The company's total income also saw a substantial increase. Significant expansion projects are underway, including new grinding units and increased clinker capacity.

news.title: Tamil Nadu: Half the land bought for new Parandur airport
news.para: The Parandur airport project in Kancheepuram district has seen significant progress with the state government acquiring 1,100 acres and disbursing ₹680 crore in compensation. While most landowners have accepted the compensation, villagers in Ekanapuram continue to oppose the project, planning to file a PIL to protect water bodies.
